articles.title: UK house prices edged down slightly in April but remains broadly stable overall

articles.title: US April Nonfarm Payrolls expected at 62K after strong March surprise
articles.expand: The United States (US) Bureau of Labor Statistics (BLS) will release the Nonfarm Payrolls (NFP) data for April on Friday at 12:30 GMT.

articles.title: Crude Oil Recovers as US May Restart Operations to Reopen the Strait of Hormuz
articles.expand: June WTI crude oil (CLM26 ) on Thursday closed down -0.27 (-0.28%), and June RBOB gasoline (RBM26 ) closed down -0.0033 (-0.10%). Crude oil and gasoline prices closed lower on Thursday on optimism that a US-Iran peace deal is imminent. The markets await further updates after the US presented a...

articles.title: Gold sticks to gains below two-week top on softer USD; focus remains on US NFP
articles.expand: Gold (XAU/USD) clings to modest intraday gains through the Asian session on Friday and remains well within striking distance of an over two-week top, touched the previous day. Despite renewed hostilities in the Strait of Hormuz, investors seem hopeful over a potential US-Iran peace deal.

articles.title: USD/INR snaps three-day losing streak as oil prices recover
articles.expand: The Indian Rupee (INR) fails to extend its three-day winning streak against the US Dollar (USD) and corrects sharply on Friday. The USD/INR pair bounces back to near 94.56 from the two-week low of 94.03 posted on Thursday.

articles.title: Chart alert: GBP/USD potential bullish reversal above 20-day moving average
articles.expand: GBP/USD is showing signs of a potential bullish reversal as sterling remains supported above key technical levels despite renewed US-Iran tensions. Traders are closely watching the US non-farm payrolls, unemployment rate, and Michigan consumer sentiment data for fresh directional cues. Intermarket analysis suggests the Bank of England may stay more...

articles.title: NFP Takes Back Seat as Hormuz Clash Keeps Markets on Edge
articles.expand: The US Non-Farm Payrolls report may be today’s headline event on the economic calendar, but markets are behaving as though the real story lies thousands of miles away in the Strait of Hormuz. For much of the past two days, investors had embraced a growing “peace trade,” betting that Washington and Tehran were moving closer […] The post NFP Ta...

articles.title: USD/CAD Price Forecast: Eases from one-week top; trades near 1.3650 ahead of jobs data
articles.expand: The USD/CAD pair struggles to build on its gains registered over the past two days and trades with a mild negative bias during the Asian session on Friday. Spot prices, however, hold above mid-1.3600s and remain close to a one-week top ahead of crucial employment details from the US and Canada.

articles.title: WTI retreats below $92.50 on US-Iran ceasefire optimism despite ongoing strikes
articles.expand: West Texas Intermediate (WTI), the US crude oil benchmark, is trading around $92.45 during the early European trading hours on Friday. The WTI price attracts some sellers on optimism over a possible deal to end the war with Iran.

articles.title: EUR/USD Price Forecast: Holds above 1.1700; Iran tensions cap gains as US NFP looms
articles.expand: The EUR/USD pair trades with a mild positive bias around the 1.1730-1.1735 region during the Asian session on Friday and, for now, seems to have stalled the previous day's modest slide.

articles.title: Asia open: Global markets jolted by US-Iran ceasefire violation, and traders brace for NFP
articles.expand: Global markets turned volatile after reports of a US-Iran ceasefire violation triggered renewed geopolitical fears ahead of the closely watched US non-farm payrolls report. Oil prices rebounded sharply above key levels, while equities pulled back from record highs as traders reassessed stagflation risks and central bank policy expectations. Asian m...

articles.title: US Nonfarm Payrolls expected to rise by 62K in April
articles.expand: The United States (US) Bureau of Labor Statistics (BLS) will release the Nonfarm Payrolls (NFP) data for April on Friday at 12:30 GMT.

articles.title: Japanese Yen gathers strength on reports of FX intervention during May holidays
articles.expand: The USD/JPY pair loses ground to around 156.85 during the Asian session on Friday. The Japanese Yen (JPY) strengthens against the US Dollar (USD) following another intervention by Japanese authorities. Markets might turn cautious later on Friday ahead of the US April employment report.

articles.title: Japan PMI Services Finalized at 51.0 as Middle East War Fuels Cost Pressures
articles.expand: Japan’s service sector growth slowed notably in April as the impact of the Middle East conflict weighed on demand and business confidence, while sharply higher costs pushed inflation pressures to near-record levels. articles.title: Japan services PMI hits 11-month low in April. War costs bite, input costs 42-month high
articles.expand: Japan's services PMI fell to 51.0 in April from 53.4, an 11-month low, as Middle East war costs drove input inflation to a 42-month high and business confidence hit its lowest since the pandemic. Summary: The S P Global Japan Services PMI fell to 51.0 in April from 53.4 in March, marking the softest rate of expansion in 11 months and a 13th consecu...

articles.title: EUR/USD edges higher above 1.1700 on US-Iran peace optimism, US NFP data looms
articles.expand: The EUR/USD pair posts modest gains around 1.1730 during the early Asian session on Monday, supported by growing optimism surrounding a potential US-Iran peace deal. Markets might turn cautious later in the day ahead of the highly-anticipated US Nonfarm Payrolls (NFP) report for April.

articles.title: Japan wage growth streak hits three months, putting June BOJ move in focus
articles.expand: Japan's real wages rose 1.0% in March, a third straight monthly gain; total cash earnings rose 2.7% against a 3.2% consensus and prior; BOJ rate decision due June 15-16. articles.title: IDR: FX curbs and bond support to steady currency – DBS

articles.expand: The AUD/USD pair attracts some buyers for the third consecutive day on Thursday and remains within striking distance of its highest level since June 2022, around the 0.7275-0.7280 region, which it touched the previous day.

articles.title: UK 30-year gilts hit 5.78%, the highest since 1998: what is being priced in?
articles.expand: UK 30-year gilts traded as high as 5.78% on Tuesday, the highest level since 1998, while 10-year yields topped 5.10% with markets pricing in nearly three-quarter-point Bank of England (BoE) rate hikes this year.

articles.expand: Swiss inflation picked up modestly in April, with headline CPI rising 0.3% month-on-month, driven largely by higher energy and travel-related costs. According to the Federal Statistical Office, increases in petrol, diesel and heating oil prices were key contributors, alongside higher airfares and international package holidays. articles.expand: The RBA has made one thing clear: it is not done yet. articles.title: EUR/USD Forex Signal: Bullish Flag Points to a Rebound
articles.expand: The EUR/USD pair has pulled back in the past few days, moving from last month's high of 1.1855 to the current 1.1720. This retreat happened after the recent Federal Reserve and the European Central Bank (ECB) interest rate decisions.
